archive.description: Bundle multiple files into a single archive

folder_structure.description: flat = all files together at the top level (default); by_job = a separate subfolder for each source (planned — not yet supported)

audio_overlay.description:
Mix a secondary audio asset (overlay) over a primary audio or
video base. Use cases: DJ tags, podcast intros/outros, station
IDs, jingles, branded mid-rolls.

**Industry naming.** This is "audio overlay" or "audio branding"
— NOT "audio watermark". Audio watermarking is steganographic
(imperceptible identifier embedded in audio for ownership /
forensic tracking) and is owned by the separate `audio_watermark`
operation (planned per ticket I20).

Multi-input (Path B with `role: base` + `role: overlay` per
the input role). `min_inputs: 2 = max_inputs: 2` for V2.0;
multi-overlay support is advertised as a planned feature
(`features.multi_overlay_stack`) but Backend implementation
deferred.

Each input is imported externally,
referenced from a vault connection, or an upstream `job_output`
(uploads are NOT referenced directly inside inputs[]). An uploaded
overlay or base enters via a `passthrough` source job referenced by
`{ type: job_output, from: <id> }` (per ticket 4som89Uh). The base
is whatever single-asset source the workflow
upstream has — for `mime_groups.audio`, the base is the primary
audio track; for `mime_groups.video`, the overlay is mixed into
the video's audio track and the visual track passes through
untouched.

`planned` — not yet supported.

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via
per-input placement options. Backend support not yet
confirmed; tagged `planned`. Mirrors the same-named feature on
`image_watermark`.

sync_to_silence.description:
Anchor overlay placement to detected silence regions in the
base track (e.g. drop a podcast jingle into the natural
breath after the host says "back in a sec"). Server-side
silence detection runs at workflow-create time; the actual
overlay start position is reported on
`OperationMetrics.overlay_anchor_ms`. Gates two extra
options: `silence_threshold_db` and `min_silence_ms`. backend
support not yet shipped.

duck_attack_ms.description:
Time (milliseconds) for the ducker to reach full
attenuation once the overlay crosses `duck_threshold`.
Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_ratio.description:
How strongly the base ducks — higher values dip it more
aggressively. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_release_ms.description:
Time (milliseconds) for the ducker to release back to
unity gain after the overlay drops below
`duck_threshold`. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_threshold.description:
Level (dB) the overlay must reach before the base audio
ducks. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duration.description:
Maximum duration (seconds) of the overlay AS PLAYED — the
overlay asset is trimmed to this length if longer. Omit
(or set 0) to play the overlay's full natural length.
**Disambiguation:** this is overlay-trim duration, NOT
output duration. The output duration always matches the
base track's duration.

fade_in_ms.description:
Linear fade-in applied to the overlay (milliseconds).
Default 0 = hard start.

fade_out_ms.description:
Linear fade-out applied to the overlay (milliseconds).
Default 0 = hard stop.

loop.description:
When true, the overlay loops continuously across the
duration of the base track (or up to the trimmed
`duration`). Useful for ambient bed music / background
stings.

loop_interval.description:
Silence gap (seconds) inserted between overlay loops.
Default 0 = back-to-back. Only applies when `loop: true`.

min_silence_ms.description:
Minimum silence duration (milliseconds) to qualify as an
anchor region for `sync_to_silence`. Inert until that
feature flips to stable.

mode.description:
How the overlay combines with the base audio:
- `mix`: both play together (overlay added on top; both audible).
- `duck`: the base audio dips while the overlay plays, then
  returns — the broadcast voice-over pattern.
- `replace`: mute the base for the overlay's duration
  (overlay only; useful for hard-cut station IDs).

overlay_gain_db.description:
Gain applied to the overlay in decibels before mixing.
Default -3 dB matches typical podcast intro / DJ-tag
level. Positive values amplify (clip risk); negative
values attenuate. Clamped to range.

silence_threshold_db.description:
Silence threshold (dB) for the `sync_to_silence` feature.
Base audio below this level for at least `min_silence_ms`
qualifies as a silence region into which the overlay can
be anchored. Inert until
`features.sync_to_silence.availability` flips to stable.

start_at.description:
Start position (seconds) on the base track at which the
overlay begins playing. Default 0.0 = mix from the
beginning. Inert when
`features.sync_to_silence.availability` flips to
`stable` and `mode: sync_to_silence` is selected
(placement is then anchored to detected silence).

duck_attack_ms.description: Ducker attack (milliseconds). Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_ratio.description: How strongly the base ducks — higher values dip it more aggressively. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_release_ms.description: Ducker release (milliseconds). Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_threshold.description: Level (dB) the overlay must reach before the base audio ducks. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duration.description:
Maximum duration (seconds) of the overlay AS PLAYED. The
overlay asset is trimmed to this length if longer. Output
video duration always matches the base.

fade_in_ms.description: Linear fade-in applied to the overlay (milliseconds).

fade_out_ms.description: Linear fade-out applied to the overlay (milliseconds).

loop.description:
When true, the overlay loops continuously across the
duration of the base video.

min_silence_ms.description:
Minimum silence duration (milliseconds) for
`sync_to_silence`. Inert until that feature flips to
stable.

mode.description:
Same modes as for audio overlays. `replace` mutes the
base video's audio for the overlay's duration.

no_audio_track_behaviour.description:
Controls behaviour when the base video has no audio
track:
- `reject`: workflow-create returns 422 (default — surfaces
  the problem at the API edge rather than producing a
  surprising silent-base output).
- `create_silent`: synthesise a silent base track at the
  video's framerate and mix the overlay into that.
- `replace`: same as `create_silent` but signals intent
  ("the overlay IS the audio track"), letting backend
  skip the base-track synthesis when the overlay covers
  the full video duration.

overlay_gain_db.description: Gain applied to the overlay in decibels before mixing.

silence_threshold_db.description:
Silence threshold (dB) for `sync_to_silence`. Inert
until that feature flips to stable.

start_at.description:
Start position (seconds) on the base video's audio track
at which the overlay begins playing.

audio_to_video.description:
Produce a video from an audio input plus an OPTIONAL still image
overlay. Use cases: podcast → YouTube uploads, audio-only tracks
that need a video container for platforms requiring it, branded
audio releases with a static cover image.

**Role asymmetry.** First role-based operation on the contract
with an OPTIONAL role — REQUIRED `base` (audio source) +
OPTIONAL `overlay` (still image, 0..1). When `overlay` is
omitted, the video uses a solid `background_color`. The
machine-readable cardinality lives in `per_role_cardinality`
on this schema.

**Input vs output asymmetry.** The single `mime_groups.audio`
block keys the INPUT MIME family (the `base` role input is
audio). The OUTPUT is always video — `output_format` controls
the container; `mime_groups` is NOT the output type. Mirrors
the `audio_overlay.video` group pattern (input-keyed).

Re-encode required (audio → video composition always re-encodes).
A lower framerate plus still-image tuning keeps file size tiny
when the overlay is a still image (the common case).

Activated to `availability: beta`: the backend is live (API
accepts + publishes), so this is shape-stable + opt-in for callers
and MUST NOT return `feature_not_available` — it may still change
with notice while beta.

background_color.description:
Hex `#RRGGBB` solid background colour. Used only when
`overlay` is omitted (no image input). When an `overlay`
image is provided, the background colour is invisible
behind the image (subject to `image_fit`).

framerate.description:
Output video framerate. Default `2` fps keeps the file size
tiny for the common case of a single still overlay. Raise for
animated overlays (not supported in V1 — overlay must be
a still image) or platform requirements (e.g. YouTube
recommends 24+ fps; some platforms accept lower).

image_fit.description:
How to fit the overlay image into the output frame.
`contain` = letterbox (preserve aspect ratio, may show
background); `cover` = crop (fill the frame, may crop
the image); `stretch` = distort to fit (preserves no
aspect ratio). Default `contain` preserves the image
aesthetic.

output_format.description:
Output container format. V1 supports `mp4` only; webm and mov
are planned additions.

output_resolution.description:
Output video resolution. `hd_720p` = 1280×720 (default),
`fhd_1080p` = 1920×1080, `sd_480p` = 854×480. Choose
lower for small file size; higher when the overlay image
quality warrants it.

audio_watermark.description:
Embed a steganographic forensic watermark into an audio asset (or
a video's audio track). The watermark is psychoacoustically masked
to be inaudible and engineered to survive lossy compression,
transcoding, broadcast chains, and analog-hole capture (Cinavia /
Resemble PerTh territory).

**Industry naming.** This is *audio watermarking* (steganographic;
used for ownership, forensic tracking, content provenance) — NOT
*audio overlay* (audible mixing of secondary audio for branding).
Audio overlay is owned by the separate `audio_overlay` operation
(per ticket I19).

Single-input. Watermark embedding takes the base asset and writes
out an indistinguishable-to-the-ear copy with the encoded payload.

**Decode endpoint.** A paired `POST /api/audio-watermark/decode`
endpoint extracts the embedded watermark from a previously-marked
asset, returning `{watermark_id, payload?, confidence, method,
detected_at}`. Decode is **own-watermarks-only** (rate-limited
+ audited; the decoder will refuse to extract from media the
caller did not mark themselves) — pin per spike S11 acceptance.

`planned` — not yet supported.

density.description:
Watermark spatial density along the asset duration:
- `sparse`: payload bits inserted at wider intervals;
  decode confidence drops on short clips.
- `normal` (default): balanced.
- `dense`: maximum decode confidence on short clips at
  the cost of slightly higher perceptual risk.

method.description:
Watermarking algorithm:
- `auto`: server selects the best available method for the
  input (currently maps to `psychoacoustic`; will widen as
  `neural` ships).
- `psychoacoustic`: hides the mark below what the ear can
  hear, so it stays inaudible. Survives MP3/AAC at moderate
  bitrates and broadcast chains.
- `neural`: machine-learning embedding. Higher survival
  under aggressive re-encoding and microphone re-recording;
  longer encoding time and non-deterministic.

payload.description:
Optional caller-supplied payload to embed (max 256
characters). Use cases: ownership marker, distribution
channel ID, license key. When omitted, the server
generates an identifier automatically and embeds that
instead; the generated ID is returned on
`OperationResult` so the caller can subsequently call
the decode endpoint to verify.

robustness.description:
Survival profile:
- `light`: minimal payload-strength; smallest perceptual
  footprint. Survives a single re-encode pass at moderate
  bitrate.
- `compression_resistant` (default): survives multiple
  MP3/AAC re-encodes at typical streaming bitrates.
- `broadcast_grade`: survives broadcast compression
  chains, EQ, dynamic-range compression, and microphone
  re-recording. Higher embedding time + slightly larger
  perceptual footprint.

density.description: Watermark density along the asset duration.

method.description:
Watermarking algorithm. Same as for an audio input;
embedded into the video's audio track only.

payload.description:
Optional caller-supplied payload to embed (max 256
characters). When omitted, the server generates an
identifier automatically and embeds that instead.

robustness.description: Survival profile. Same as for an audio input.

compress.description: Reduce file size while maintaining acceptable quality

bitrate.description: Output bitrate in kbps (CBR/ABR). Applies to lossy OUTPUTS. Only a caller-EXPLICIT bitrate against a lossless output is rejected as invalid_options (flac/wav have no bitrate target); the `default: 128` is never the basis for a rejection (a default a caller did not set is the server's to resolve, and is dropped for a lossless target). The output is the input format unless `output_format` re-targets it: with `output_format: original` the lossy/lossless distinction follows the input MIME (mpeg/aac/ogg/mp4 = lossy → bitrate applies; flac/wav input → an explicit bitrate is rejected); with an explicit `output_format`, it follows that target (flac/wav target → an explicit bitrate is rejected). Not expressed as `depends_on` — the lossy-output condition is an output-value × input-MIME cross-axis the option-to-option `depends_on` cannot model without dropping `original` and breaking same-format callers. The other knobs (channels/sample_rate/normalize) apply to all formats.

channels.description: Number of audio channels (1 = mono, 2 = stereo)

normalize.description: Apply loudness normalization (EBU R128 / -14 LUFS target)

output_format.description: Output audio format. original = keep input format (default, same-format compression). Non-`original` values re-target the container/codec via the API "compress+format" facade (one canonicalized convert pass); see FORMAT.md.

trim_end.description: Trim from end in seconds (absent = to the end). Removes this many seconds from the end; with `trim_start` the kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`]. See `trim_start`.

trim_start.description: Trim from beginning in seconds (absent = from the start). The clip is cut before encoding, with the same from-end semantic as compress.video / convert / merge: the kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`].

font_subsetting.description: Subset embedded fonts to only include glyphs used in the document. Significant size reduction for books with large font files. `planned` — not yet supported.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest, 100 = best). The only document compression option supported today.

strip_unused_css.description: Remove CSS rules not referenced by any content. Risk: may affect rendering in some readers. `planned` — not yet supported.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest, 100 = best). The only document compression option supported today.

strip_metadata.description: Remove document metadata (author, revision history, comments). `planned` — not yet supported.

strip_unused_styles.description: Remove style definitions not referenced in document content. `planned` — not yet supported.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest, 100 = best). The only document compression option supported today.

strip_hidden_data.description: Remove revisions, comments, personal info, hidden rows/columns, and speaker notes. `planned` — not yet supported.

strip_macros.description: Remove VBA macros and ActiveX controls. `planned` — not yet supported.

strip_unused_fonts.description: Remove embedded fonts not referenced in the document. Risk: may affect rendering on systems without the font installed. `planned` — not yet supported.

colorspace.description: Output color space. `planned` — not yet supported (use `grayscale` for grayscale conversion).

flatten_forms.description: Flatten interactive form fields into static content. `planned` — not yet supported.

grayscale.description: Convert all images to grayscale (smaller output; discards colour).

image_dpi.description: Downsample images above this DPI (72–600 inclusive). Omit to use automatic DPI analysis. Out-of-range is rejected.

pages.description: Page selection (e.g. '1-5,8,10-12'). Omit to keep all pages. `planned` — not yet supported.

profile.description: Compression preset, pinned directly (overrides the quality→preset mapping). screen = lowest-res / smallest, ebook = mid, printer = 300dpi, prepress = print-production. Optional — omit to let `quality` drive the preset.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest, 100 = best). Maps to a compression preset. Overridden by `profile` when that is set.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels (honour the camera/device rotation tag) so the output displays upright everywhere, then drop the now-redundant orientation tag. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag. On an already-rotated image the pixels are re-encoded (near-lossless), which can drop other (non-orientation) metadata even with `metadata: keep`.

color_profile.description: Colour-profile handling — a dedicated control, separate from `metadata` so that stripping metadata never silently shifts colours. `keep` (default) = preserve your colours: embed the ICC profile where the output format can carry one, otherwise convert to sRGB and bake the colours in so they survive (never a silent drop — for TIFF inputs the gamut is baked to sRGB; GIF carries no profile so this is a no-op); `srgb` = convert to sRGB (transform pixels to sRGB, emit untagged — no embedded ICC, the web default; CMYK/grayscale best-effort); `strip` = drop the profile (smallest — only when the consumer manages colour itself). On compress `srgb` is `planned` for this group (the sRGB pixel transform is wired for JPEG/PNG only so far); `keep` and `strip` are live.

fit.description: Resize mode. `max` (default) = fit within the given bounds, preserving aspect ratio and never upscaling (caps at the source dimensions — this is the "no-enlarge" behaviour). `crop`/`scale` = exact dimensions (`scale` deliberately enlarges past the source). Applies only when width or height is set.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height within the group's max output area). Resizes the image (one output). Optional — see `width`.

metadata.description: Metadata handling. `strip` = remove all EXIF/IPTC/XMP (default). `keep` = preserve EXIF, colour profile, and XMP. `all` is a DEPRECATED alias of `strip` (kept for older callers; emits Deprecation/Sunset headers — migrate to `strip`). Any other value is rejected.

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format (default); webp = recompress to WebP. Concrete format changes (jpeg/png/avif/gif/tiff) are handled by the convert operation. `auto` / `smallest` are PLANNED auto-format directives — the server encodes to several candidate formats and returns the best (`auto` = the most suitable; `smallest` = the absolute fewest bytes), reporting the chosen format via the result `mime_type`. They are selection strategies, not concrete targets, and stay `planned` until supported. See FORMAT.md.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest file, 100 = best quality). For animated GIF this is honoured but real-world size reduction is typically small.

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Resizes the image as part of the operation (one transformed output, replacing the source). Optional; omit to keep the source dimensions.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels (honour the camera/device rotation tag) so the output displays upright everywhere, then drop the now-redundant orientation tag. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag. On an already-rotated image the pixels are re-encoded (near-lossless), which can drop other (non-orientation) metadata even with `metadata: keep`.

avif_speed.description: AVIF encode speed (1-10). Lower = smaller file, slower encode; higher = faster, larger. Default 4.

color_profile.description: Colour-profile handling — a dedicated control, separate from `metadata` so that stripping metadata never silently shifts colours. `keep` (default) = preserve your colours: embed the ICC profile where the output format can carry one, otherwise convert to sRGB and bake the colours in so they survive (never a silent drop — for AVIF the gamut is baked to sRGB); `srgb` = convert to sRGB (transform pixels to sRGB, emit untagged — no embedded ICC, the web default; CMYK/grayscale best-effort); `strip` = drop the profile (smallest — only when the consumer manages colour itself). All three values are live on compress AVIF — `srgb` flipped planned->stable 2026-06-30 on the lambdas proof (wide-gamut-p3-icc.avif: srgb bakes untagged, whole-image RMSE 0.041 / 92% pixels shifted vs strip). Note: on AVIF `keep` and `srgb` both bake the gamut to sRGB by design, so keep_px == srgb_px; the proving inequality is srgb != strip, which holds.

encoding_mode.description: Compression mode. `quality` = the `quality` slider drives the encode (default). `target_size` = automatically find the highest quality that fits a byte budget (set via `target_size_bytes`); if the budget can't be met even at the lowest quality, the closest result is returned, and the original is kept if re-encoding wouldn't be smaller. `auto_quality` = hit a named perceptual-quality target (set via `quality_preset`) and let the worker pick the encoder quality that meets it — the perceptual twin of `target_size` (which targets bytes); the result reports the chosen quality. AVIF encoding is slower, so the search is bounded and may stop slightly above the budget.

fit.description: Resize mode. `max` (default) = fit within the given bounds, preserving aspect ratio and never upscaling (caps at the source dimensions — this is the "no-enlarge" behaviour). `crop`/`scale` = exact dimensions (`scale` deliberately enlarges past the source). Applies only when width or height is set.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height within the group's max output area). Resizes the image (one output). Optional — see `width`.

metadata.description: Metadata handling. `strip` = remove all EXIF/IPTC/XMP (default, the only supported behaviour). `all` is a DEPRECATED alias of `strip` (migrate to `strip`). `keep` is NOT offered for AVIF — it is re-encoded from pixels and cannot preserve metadata.

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format (default); webp = recompress to WebP. Concrete format changes (jpeg/png/avif/gif/tiff) are handled by the convert operation. `auto` / `smallest` are PLANNED auto-format directives — the server encodes to several candidate formats and returns the best (`auto` = the most suitable; `smallest` = the absolute fewest bytes), reporting the chosen format via the result `mime_type`. They are selection strategies, not concrete targets, and stay `planned` until supported. See FORMAT.md.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest file, 100 = best quality). Active in `quality` mode (default); in `target_size` mode the quality is chosen automatically to hit the size budget, so this option is inactive.

quality_preset.description: Named perceptual-quality target for `encoding_mode: auto_quality` (best > good > fair > low; `good` is the default). The worker runs a perceptual-target search and picks the encoder quality that meets the named tier — the wire carries the NAME only, so the tier→target mapping stays worker-internal and calibration-tunable without a contract change. Requires `encoding_mode: auto_quality`. The achieved perceptual score is reported on the result as `metrics.measured_quality` (+ `metrics.quality_metric`).

target_size_bytes.description: Target output size in bytes (min 1024 = 1 KiB). The encoder searches for the highest quality that lands at or under this size; if the target can't be met even at the lowest quality, the closest result is returned (the result reports the chosen quality and whether the target was met). Requires `encoding_mode: target_size`.

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Resizes the image as part of the operation (one transformed output, replacing the source). Optional; omit to keep the source dimensions.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels (honour the camera/device rotation tag) so the output displays upright everywhere, then drop the now-redundant orientation tag. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag. On an already-rotated image the pixels are re-encoded (near-lossless), which can drop other (non-orientation) metadata even with `metadata: keep`.

chroma_subsampling.description: JPEG chroma subsampling — the colour-resolution/size trade-off. `420` (4:2:0) = smallest, chroma at quarter resolution (typical for web photos); `422` (4:2:2) = half horizontal resolution; `444` (4:4:4) = full chroma (largest, best for sharp colour edges / text). JPEG only. INCOMPATIBLE with `lossless` (any chroma value): a lossless JPEG copies the existing coefficients and cannot resample chroma.

lossless.description: Lossless JPEG re-optimisation: re-packs the file WITHOUT re-encoding the pixels — no quality loss (it cannot recover detail a lossy JPEG already lost). Only in `quality` mode — incompatible with `target_size` (hitting a byte budget is inherently lossy).

progressive.description: Enable progressive JPEG rendering (a low-quality preview renders first, then refines). JPEG only.

Applies only when width or height is set.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height within the group's max output area). Resizes the image (one output). Optional — see `width`.

metadata.description: Metadata handling. `strip` = remove all EXIF/IPTC/XMP (default). `keep` = preserve EXIF, colour profile, and XMP. `all` is a DEPRECATED alias of `strip` (kept for older callers; emits Deprecation/Sunset headers — migrate to `strip`). Any other value is rejected.

optimization_level.description: PNG optimization effort (0-6). Higher = smaller file, slower encode. Default 3.

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format (default); webp = recompress to WebP. Concrete format changes (jpeg/png/avif/gif/tiff) are handled by the convert operation. `auto` / `smallest` are PLANNED auto-format directives — the server encodes to several candidate formats and returns the best (`auto` = the most suitable; `smallest` = the absolute fewest bytes), reporting the chosen format via the result `mime_type`. They are selection strategies, not concrete targets, and stay `planned` until supported. See FORMAT.md.

quality.description: Compression quality (1-100). For PNG this is the SINGLE lossy/lossless control: `quality` < 100 applies lossy palette quantization (imagequant — licence-cleared for our server-side SaaS backend, `kkajDFXD`; the value is the quantization level), while `quality: 100` keeps the PNG lossless (oxipng optimisation only — tune the lossless effort via `optimization_level`). 1 = smallest file, 100 = best quality / lossless. There is no separate lossy/lossless toggle for PNG — it is purely quality-driven.

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Resizes the image as part of the operation (one transformed output, replacing the source). Optional; omit to keep the source dimensions.

metadata.description: Metadata handling. `strip` = remove all metadata (default, the only supported behaviour). `all` is a DEPRECATED alias of `strip` (migrate to `strip`). `keep` is NOT offered for SVG — optimising the markup cannot preserve a metadata block.

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format (default); webp = recompress to WebP. Concrete format changes are handled by the convert operation. `auto` / `smallest` are PLANNED auto-format directives (the server picks the best/smallest candidate format, reported via the result `mime_type`) — selection strategies, not concrete targets, planned until supported. See FORMAT.md.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels (honour the camera/device rotation tag) so the output displays upright everywhere, then drop the now-redundant orientation tag. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag. On an already-rotated image the pixels are re-encoded (near-lossless), which can drop other (non-orientation) metadata even with `metadata: keep`.

color_profile.description: Colour-profile handling — a dedicated control, separate from `metadata` so that stripping metadata never silently shifts colours. `keep` (default) = preserve your colours: embed the ICC profile where the output format can carry one, otherwise convert to sRGB and bake the colours in so they survive (never a silent drop); `srgb` = convert to sRGB (transform pixels to sRGB, emit untagged — no embedded ICC, the web default; CMYK/grayscale best-effort); `strip` = drop the profile (smallest — only when the consumer manages colour itself). All three values are live on the WebP same-format route — the sRGB pixel transform is staging-proven (lambdas compression-image git-13b5000: srgb output untagged + a real pixel shift vs strip).

encoding_mode.description: Compression mode. `quality` = the `quality` slider drives the encode (default). `target_size` = automatically find the highest quality that fits a byte budget (set via `target_size_bytes`); if the budget can't be met even at the lowest quality, the closest result is returned, and the original is kept if re-encoding wouldn't be smaller. `auto_quality` = hit a named perceptual-quality target (set via `quality_preset`) and let the worker pick the encoder quality that meets it — the perceptual twin of `target_size` (which targets bytes); the result reports the chosen quality.

fit.description: Resize mode. `max` (default) = fit within the given bounds, preserving aspect ratio and never upscaling (caps at the source dimensions — this is the "no-enlarge" behaviour). `crop`/`scale` = exact dimensions (`scale` deliberately enlarges past the source). Applies only when width or height is set.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height within the group's max output area). Resizes the image (one output). Optional — see `width`.

lossless.description: Encode WebP losslessly: a genuine lossless WebP encode. Note a lossless encode can be LARGER than a lossy one at the same visual quality. Only in `quality` mode — incompatible with `target_size` (hitting a byte budget is inherently lossy).

metadata.description: Metadata handling. `strip` = remove all EXIF/IPTC/XMP (default). `keep` = preserve EXIF, colour profile, and XMP. `all` is a DEPRECATED alias of `strip` (kept for older callers; emits Deprecation/Sunset headers — migrate to `strip`). Any other value is rejected.

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format (default); webp = recompress to WebP. Concrete format changes (jpeg/png/avif/gif/tiff) are handled by the convert operation. `auto` / `smallest` are PLANNED auto-format directives — the server encodes to several candidate formats and returns the best (`auto` = the most suitable; `smallest` = the absolute fewest bytes), reporting the chosen format via the result `mime_type`. They are selection strategies, not concrete targets, and stay `planned` until supported. See FORMAT.md.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest file, 100 = best quality). Active in `quality` mode (default); in `target_size` mode the quality is chosen automatically to hit the size budget, so this option is inactive.

quality_preset.description: Named perceptual-quality target for `encoding_mode: auto_quality` (best > good > fair > low; `good` is the default). The worker runs a perceptual-target search and picks the encoder quality that meets the named tier — the wire carries the NAME only, so the tier→target mapping stays worker-internal and calibration-tunable without a contract change. Requires `encoding_mode: auto_quality`. The achieved perceptual score is reported on the result as `metrics.measured_quality` (+ `metrics.quality_metric`).

target_size_bytes.description: Target output size in bytes (min 1024 = 1 KiB). The encoder searches for the highest quality that lands at or under this size; if the target can't be met even at the lowest quality, the closest result is returned (the result reports the chosen quality and whether the target was met). Requires `encoding_mode: target_size`.

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Resizes the image as part of the operation (one transformed output, replacing the source). Optional; omit to keep the source dimensions.

audio_bitrate.description: Audio track bitrate in kbps. Only valid with an explicit re-encode audio_codec (aac/opus/vorbis): an explicit audio_bitrate with audio_codec `copy`/omitted is rejected; copy preserves the source bitrate. No default: a bitrate is only meaningful on an explicit re-encode.

audio_codec.description: Audio codec. copy = passthrough (keep the source stream as-is; re-encode only on explicit request) — aac/opus/vorbis re-encode. Default is `copy`: on the same-container path (`output_format` original/absent) the source stream is already container-compatible, so copy is safe. When `output_format` RE-TARGETS the container, an absent/default audio_codec resolves to a container-valid codec via the facade (e.g. webm -> opus), NOT a blind copy of an incompatible stream — a DEFAULT is never rejected (the exact per-container resolution, incl. ogg vorbis-vs-opus, is an output_format stable-flip prerequisite). A caller-EXPLICIT codec the resolved container cannot carry (e.g. aac on a webm target) is rejected. See FORMAT.md "Container-conditional defaults".

codec.description: Video codec. h264 = widest compatibility, h265/av1 = better compression but slower. The default (h264) is the MP4/MOV standard and is CONTAINER-CONDITIONAL against the RESOLVED output container (the explicit `output_format` if set, else the input container): for a container that cannot carry it (e.g. WebM, which carries VP8/VP9/ AV1) the server resolves a container-valid codec (WebM -> vp9). A DEFAULT value is never rejected — including an explicit `output_format: webm` left at the default `h264` (resolves to vp9, not rejected); only a caller-EXPLICIT incompatible codec is rejected. See FORMAT.md "Container-conditional defaults".

crf.description: Constant Rate Factor (0 = lossless, 51 = worst, 23 = visually good). Only applies to CRF mode.

encoding_mode.description: crf = constant quality (variable file size). target_size = constrained output file size (two-pass). target_size is LIVE for mp4/MOV output; for webm/ogg output and long-form the server returns `feature_not_available` (use crf there) until two-pass for this container ships.

faststart.description: Move the MP4/MOV moov atom to the front so the file starts playing before it is fully downloaded (progressive web playback). MP4/MOV only. CONTAINER-CONDITIONAL against the RESOLVED output container (the explicit `output_format` if set, else the input): the default (true) is silently inapplicable for a container with no moov atom (e.g. WebM) — that is NOT a rejection. Only a caller-EXPLICIT faststart:true on such a container is rejected. See FORMAT.md "Container-conditional defaults".

fit.description: Resize mode. max = fit within bounds (preserves ratio), crop/scale/pad = exact dimensions. Only applies when width or height is set.

height.description: Output height in pixels. Must be an even integer ≥ 2; odd values are rejected by the encoder.

output_format.description: Output container. original = keep the input container (default, same-format compression). Non-`original` values re-target the container via the API "compress+format" facade (one canonicalized convert-with-size-cap pass); see FORMAT.md. When set, the RESOLVED target container determines the valid codec/audio_codec/faststart sets — an absent/default codec resolves to a container-valid one (e.g. webm -> vp9) and is never rejected; only a caller-EXPLICIT incompatible value is rejected.

preset.description: Encoding speed vs compression ratio trade-off

target_size_bytes.description: Target output file size in bytes (min 1MiB). A two-pass encode lands the output at or under the target (a safety undershoot + audio reserve apply). mp4/MOV output only — webm/ogg + long-form return `feature_not_available` until two-pass for this container ships.

trim_end.description: Trim from end in seconds (absent = to the end). Removes this many seconds from the end; with `trim_start` the kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`]. See `trim_start` (short-form only; long-form is not supported).

trim_start.description: Trim from beginning in seconds (absent = from the start). The clip is cut before encoding, with the same from-end semantic as convert/merge: the kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`]. SHORT-FORM only — for long-form video this is not supported (trim a short clip, or chain a `split`); see the compress.video.trim.longform constraint.

width.description: Output width in pixels. Must be an even integer ≥ 2; odd values are rejected by the encoder.

convert.description: Convert file to a different format

bitrate.description: Output bitrate in kbps (lossy formats only — mp3/aac/ogg/opus)

output_format.description: Target image format (renders PDF pages as images)

pages.description:
Page selection (e.g. '1-5,8'). Each resolved page produces a
separate output file. **Maximum 200 resolved entries per
request** — aligns with the `OperationResult.outputs[]`
`maxItems: 200` cap. Expressions resolving to more than 200
entries (e.g. `'1-201'`, `'1-100,102-205'`) are out of
contract. Duplicate or overlapping entries (e.g. `'1,1'`,
`'1-100,50-150'`) are counted toward the cap as parsed;
deduplication, if any, is a runtime concern and is NOT part
of this contract.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels (honour the camera/device rotation tag) so the output displays upright everywhere, then drop the now-redundant orientation tag. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag. The format-change re-encodes from pixels, so other (non-orientation) metadata is not carried across.

background.description: Background colour as a 6-digit hex (`#RRGGBB`, e.g. `#ffffff`) for transparent images converted to JPEG (the alpha-flatten fill colour when converting to JPEG). HEX ONLY: a non-hex value (e.g. a CSS named colour) is rejected rather than silently falling back.

color_profile.description: Colour-profile handling — a dedicated control, separate from `metadata` so that stripping metadata never silently shifts colours. `keep` (default) = preserve your colours: embed the ICC profile where the target format can carry one, otherwise convert to sRGB and bake the colours in so they survive (never a silent drop); `srgb` = convert to sRGB (transform pixels to sRGB, emit untagged — no embedded ICC, the web default; CMYK/grayscale best-effort); `strip` = drop the profile. The worker reads the input ICC for every accepted raster input (JPEG/PNG/WebP/TIFF, plus HEIF/AVIF via the ISO-BMFF `colr` box).

fit.description: Resize mode. `max` (default) = fit within the given bounds, preserving aspect ratio and never upscaling (caps at the source dimensions — this is the "no-enlarge" behaviour). `crop`/`scale` = exact dimensions (`scale` deliberately enlarges past the source). Applies only when width or height is set.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Not honored for SVG input. See `width`.

metadata.description: Metadata handling across a format change. `strip` = remove all EXIF/IPTC/XMP (default), `keep` = preserve. `planned` — metadata is not yet preserved across a format change. The honoured target subset may narrow (avif/heic targets are re-encoded and cannot keep metadata).

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Resizes the image before encoding (one transformed output). NOT honored for SVG input. Optional; omit to keep the source dimensions.

auto_orient.description: Bake EXIF orientation into the pixels so the output displays upright everywhere. Default `true`; no-op for inputs without an orientation tag (a GIF carries none, so this is a no-op for GIF input). Scoped to the STILL-IMAGE targets only — the video targets (mp4/webm) carry no EXIF orientation.

background.description: Background colour as a 6-digit hex (`#RRGGBB`) for a transparent GIF flattened to JPEG (alpha-flatten). HEX ONLY — a non-hex value is rejected (see convert.image.background).

color_profile.description: Colour-profile handling — `keep` (default) = preserve your colours (embed where the target carries an ICC, else bake to sRGB; a no-op for the GIF input itself, which carries no profile); `srgb` = convert to sRGB (transform pixels to sRGB, emit untagged — no embedded ICC, the web default); `strip` = drop the profile. Scoped to the STILL-IMAGE targets only — the video targets (mp4/webm) carry no colour profile, so this is not accepted for a gif→video output.

fit.description: Resize mode. `max` (default) = fit within bounds, preserving aspect ratio and never upscaling; `crop`/`scale` = exact dimensions. Applies only when width or height is set — and width/height are still-image-only here, so fit is transitively unreachable for the mp4/webm video targets.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Scoped to the still-image targets — see `width`.

output_format.description: Target format. The still-image targets (jpeg/png/webp/avif/gif/tiff) take the first frame / rasterise. `mp4` / `webm` are the GIF→VIDEO transcode targets — the animated GIF becomes a muted, play-once video (`mp4` = H.264, plays everywhere; `webm` = VP9, smaller), typically far smaller than the original GIF. GIFs are silent (no audio stream); the GIF infinite-loop becomes a playback attribute (`<video loop>`) the player sets, NOT baked into the file.

quality.description: Output quality for the lossy STILL-IMAGE targets (jpeg/webp/avif). The video targets (mp4/webm) use a sensible default encode quality in v1 — there is no exposed CRF knob yet (may be added later).

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width × height must not exceed the group's maximum output area). Scoped to the STILL-IMAGE targets only — resizing while transcoding to the mp4/webm video targets is not yet supported, so width/height are not accepted for the video targets.

background.description: Background colour as a 6-digit hex (`#RRGGBB`) for transparent SVG rasterised to JPEG (alpha-flatten). HEX ONLY — a non-hex value is rejected (see convert.image.background).

output_format.description: Target raster format (SVG is rasterised, then encoded).

crf.description: CRF (Constant Rate Factor): 0 = lossless/best, 51 = worst. Lower = higher quality + larger file (the inverse of image `quality`). Video outputs only — NOT applicable to a `gif` output (frame extraction has no CRF). An explicit `crf` on a `gif` output is rejected as `invalid_options` at workflow-create (the API enforces this `depends_on`); FE/SDK prune it pre-submit.

dither.description: GIF dithering method. `bayer` is a good size/quality balance; `none` is smallest (may band).

fps.description: Output frame rate for video → GIF. Lower = fewer frames = smaller file. Video → GIF only.

loop.description: GIF loop count: 0 = infinite (default), N > 0 = loop N times, -1 = play once.

max_colors.description: GIF palette size (the palette generation max colours). Fewer colors = smaller file.

output_format.description: Target format. The video codec is fixed by the container (mp4->H.264, webm->VP9, ogg->Theora, gif->palette, mov->H.264, mkv->H.264) — not caller-selectable.

trim_end.description: Trim from end in seconds (absent = to the end of the clip). Removes this many seconds from the end; combined with `trim_start` the kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`]. Live — see `trim_start`.

trim_start.description: Trim from beginning in seconds (absent = from the start). The clip is cut before transcoding — the user's "trim a video then make a GIF" path. The kept clip is [`trim_start`, input_duration − `trim_end`]. Live for all video outputs.

width.description: Maximum output width in pixels — a downscale-only CAP (a source narrower than this is left untouched; height is aspect-preserved). Smaller = smaller file.

custom_luma.description:
Apply a caller-uploaded luma matte (transition-mask) over a base
video to produce a custom luma-matte transition effect. Inspired
by Cloudinary's deprecated luma-matte custom transitions and
intended as a paid-tier (`pro`+) creative feature.

**NOT** related to the `xfade=custom` expression-mode transition
parameter. The `merge.transition.custom` value is tagged `planned`
as an advisory that points callers at this dedicated operation for
caller-uploaded luma-matte transitions.

Multi-input: exactly one input with `role: base` (the source
video being transitioned) + exactly one input with
`role: transition_mask` (the luma matte — a video whose pixel
luminance drives the transition reveal — bright = revealed,
dark = held). Each input is imported
externally, referenced from a vault connection, or an upstream
`job_output` (uploads are NOT referenced directly inside inputs[]).
An uploaded base or mask enters via a `passthrough` source job
referenced by `{ type: job_output, from: <id> }` (per ticket
4som89Uh).

`planned` — not yet supported.

duration.description:
Transition duration in seconds. The mask is time-stretched
or trimmed to this duration when applied.

mask_blur.description:
Gaussian blur radius (pixels) applied to the luma matte
before compositing. Softens the transition edge. Default
0.0 = no blur. Larger values produce a more diffused
transition.

mask_contrast.description:
Contrast multiplier applied to the luma matte before
compositing. Sharpens (>1) or softens (<1) the
transition edge. Default 1.0 = no contrast adjustment.

mask_invert.description:
When true, inverts the luma matte (bright ↔ dark). Lets
callers reuse a single mask asset for both reveal and
hide directions.

start_offset.description:
Offset (seconds) from the base clip start at which the
transition begins. The transition runs for `duration`
seconds starting at `start_offset`.

image_watermark.description:
Apply an image overlay onto a base media
asset. Multi-input: exactly one input with role: base (the source
asset) + exactly one with role: overlay (the watermark). Each input
is an external_import handle, a vault
connection, or an upstream `job_output` (uploads are NOT referenced
directly inside inputs[]). To use an uploaded base or overlay, feed
it through a `passthrough` source job and reference that job via
`{ type: job_output, from: <id> }` (per ticket 4som89Uh).

Stable today for static-image bases (image/jpeg, image/png,
image/webp); animated GIF bases are advertised as `planned` via
the parallel `image_gif` mime_group — schema is contract-defined,
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until backend
support ships.

TIFF (image/tiff) and BMP (image/bmp) bases are now `stable` too
(flipped planned->stable 2026-06-30 on the lambdas staging proof,
UANjD38A / #296) via the parallel `image_tiff` / `image_bmp`
mime_groups. Because watermark output mirrors the input format
(`produces: same_as_input`), advertising a base format is only honest
when the worker can both decode AND encode it — which holds for
TIFF/BMP but not for HEIC/AVIF (no encoder) or SVG (cannot re-emit svg
from a rasterised composite); those stay out of scope.

**Video bases are NOT supported by `image_watermark`.** Use the
dedicated `video_watermark` operation
(`schemas/operations/video_watermark.yaml`), which overlays and
re-encodes the video.

**Audio is explicitly NOT supported.** For audio overlay (DJ tags,
podcast intros, jingles), use the `audio_overlay` operation
(declared in schemas/operations/audio_overlay.yaml at
`availability: planned`).

Source capped at 100 megapixels for image bases; per-frame caps for
animated GIF bases will be defined when the corresponding backend
support ships.

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via per-input placement options.
Backend support not yet confirmed; tagged planned. Scope is
image-base only at the time this feature was specified; extension
to GIF / video bases tracks alongside the corresponding mime_group
`planned` → `stable` flips.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position on the base image. Combined with
margin_x and margin_y for fine-grained offset from the
anchor point.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as either pixels (e.g.
"40px") or percentage of base image width (e.g. "5%").
Direction is anchor-relative — positive moves toward image
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** When the anchor is `center`
the centre is the centre — there is no anchor-relative
direction. Non-zero `margin_x` (anything other than the
default `"0px"` or the equivalent `"0%"`) combined with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422). To nudge a
roughly-centred overlay, pick a peripheral anchor (e.g.
`top_left`) and use `margin_x` / `margin_y` to position it.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as either pixels (e.g.
"20px") or percentage of base image height (e.g. "3%").
Direction is anchor-relative — positive moves toward image
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as
`margin_x` — non-zero `margin_y` with `anchor: center` is
rejected by the API at workflow-create time as
`invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

opacity.description:
Overlay opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).
Clamped to range.

overlay_width.description:
Optional overlay width as either pixels (e.g. "200px") or
percentage of base image width (e.g. "30%"). Aspect ratio is
preserved. Omit to use the original overlay size.

gap_duration.description: Silence duration between clips in seconds

normalize_audio.description: Normalize audio levels across all clips

output_type.description: Output format for audio merge (concatenation). Currently fixed at 'audio'. Optional — API injects this default post-validation so callers may omit it.

transition.description:
Transition between audio clips. `none` joins them with a
hard cut; `crossfade` blends one into the next.

crossfade_duration.description: Override crossfade duration for this join point

gap_duration.description: Override gap duration for this join point

transition.description:
Override transition for the join point preceding this
input. Audio-only catalog (acrossfade-backed); see
FORMAT.md §"Audio vs visual transition catalogs (merge)".

trim_end.description:
Trim from end in seconds (per-input). Applied to this input
BEFORE any transition/crossfade. Matches the `compress` trim
semantic. No `max` — the upper bound is the input's own
duration, enforced at processing time, not statically bounded
here.

trim_start.description:
Trim from beginning in seconds (per-input). Applied to this
input BEFORE any transition/crossfade. Matches the
`compress` trim semantic. No `max` — the upper bound is the
input's own duration, enforced at processing time, not statically
bounded here.

output_type.description: Result format: animated GIF or slideshow video. No default — caller must pick explicitly (image/collage and document/PDF concat are not supported by V1 merge).

transition.description:
Transition effect between images in the slideshow output.
`none` means a hard cut with no transition. Some effects
are still rolling out — check their availability.

transition_duration.description: Duration of transition effect in seconds

codec.description:
Output codec when merge re-encodes. Inert when stream-copy
concat is selected (re_encode_mode=auto + compatible inputs).

crf.description: Constant Rate Factor when encoding_mode=crf. Lower = higher quality / larger file.

encoding_mode.description:
CRF aims for a quality level and lets the file size vary;
target_size aims for a fixed output file size. `target_size`
currently works with `codec=h264` only — a request pairing it
with another codec is rejected as `invalid_options`.

normalize_audio.description: Normalize audio levels across all clips

output_type.description: Output format for video merge (concatenation). Currently fixed at 'video'. Optional — API injects this default post-validation so callers may omit it.

re_encode_mode.description:
Re-encode policy for video concat.
- `auto`: probe inputs and choose stream-copy concat (when
  codec/container/timebase/framerate/resolution/pixel-format
  match) or full re-encode.
- `always`: force re-encode (use when caller wants
  normalised output options).
- `never`: return 422 `REQUIRES_REENCODE` if inputs are not
  concat-compatible.
Compatibility algorithm is server-side and deliberately
opaque (per plan v5 §F8.2). The actual path taken is
reported on `OperationMetrics.re_encode_decision`.

target_resolution.description: Output dimensions WxH (e.g. "1920x1080"). Omit to inherit from inputs.

target_size_bytes.description:
Target file size in bytes (minimum 1 MiB). Required when
`encoding_mode=target_size`, which is h264-only.

Practical floor: if the target works out to less than
~100 kbps of video (after subtracting the ~160 kbps AAC audio
track over the merged duration), the encoder floors it and the
resulting file will not honour the requested `target_size_bytes`.
Publishers targeting very small outputs should size accordingly.

transition.description:
Default transition between clips. Some effects are still
rolling out — check their availability.

crossfade_duration.description: Override crossfade duration for this join point

transition.description: Override transition for the join point preceding this input.

trim_end.description:
Trim from end in seconds (per-input). Applied to this input
BEFORE any transition/crossfade. Matches the `compress` trim
semantic. No `max` — the upper bound is the input's own
duration, enforced at processing time, not statically bounded
here.

trim_start.description:
Trim from beginning in seconds (per-input). Applied to this
input BEFORE any transition/crossfade. Matches the
`compress` trim semantic. No `max` — the upper bound is the
input's own duration, enforced at processing time, not statically
bounded here.

passthrough.description:
Inert lossless source operation. A single-input source job whose
SOLE operation is `passthrough` emits its source bytes UNCHANGED —
no compression, no transformation, no backend dispatch. The API
self-completes the job at workflow-create/publish time: the job's
terminal output IS the upload's `{bucket, key}` unchanged.

**Purpose.** Lets an uploaded file enter a multi-input operation
(`merge` / `archive` / `image_watermark` / etc.) LOSSLESSLY. Since
V2 narrows the input source to exclude upload-direct (a multi-input source
— job_output / external_import / connection only), an upload that
must feed a multi-input op enters via a `passthrough` source job
referenced downstream by `{ type: job_output, from: <id> }`. This
preserves billing / DAG / lineage that direct upload-in-inputs[]
would bypass.

**Distinct from `operations: []`.** An empty `operations[]` on a
single-input upload job KEEPS its implicit-compress meaning (see
`POST /api/workflows` description). `passthrough` is the EXPLICIT
lossless opt-out chosen via the existing rule "non-empty
`operations[]` without `compress` = compression opt-out" — it is a
sole, named operation, not an empty chain.

**Never published to SNS.** Because the API self-completes the job
at publish, `passthrough` is INTENTIONALLY absent from the AsyncAPI
routing enums (`OperationType`, `OperationRequestAttributes.operation_type`)
and has no `ops-passthrough` queue. It never appears on the wire as
a request message.

Media-agnostic (no `mime_groups`): it accepts any uploaded MIME and
copies it verbatim, so there are no per-MIME options or constraints.
`availability: beta` — the API self-completion path is LIVE (the
inputs[]-narrowing + passthrough self-complete mechanism is deployed),
so workflow-create accepts `passthrough` source jobs and MUST NOT
return `feature_not_available`. Activated per ticket
[`4som89Uh`](https://trello.com/c/4som89Uh) (planned→beta flip;
self-complete code deployed API-side).

render_variants.description: Generate multiple resized / re-encoded image variants from a single source image in one operation (image fan-out).

targets.description:
One output variant per entry (max 20). Each variant is resized /
re-encoded independently from the single decoded source; the
operation succeeds or fails **atomically** — there are no partial
outputs. Each result output echoes its target's `id` back as
`target_id` so callers can map outputs to the variants they asked
for.

Dimensions: `fit: max` needs at least one of `width` / `height`
(aspect preserved, output may be smaller than the box). `fit: crop`
and `fit: scale` require BOTH `width` and `height` (they produce an
exact-size output). A target whose dimensions exceed the source is
rejected (`invalid_options`) unless `allow_upscale: true`.

allow_upscale.description: When false (default), a target whose dimensions exceed the source is rejected (`invalid_options`). Set true to permit upscaling. fit max never upscales.

background.description: Background colour (hex #RRGGBB), applied only when flattening a transparent source into an opaque format (e.g. a PNG with alpha rendered to JPEG). Ignored otherwise.

fit.description:
Resize mode. max = fit within the box, aspect preserved
(output may be smaller); needs at least one dimension.
crop = fill and trim to exact width x height. scale = force
exact width x height. crop and scale require both dimensions.

height.description: Target height in pixels. Required together with width for fit crop/scale; for fit max, at least one of width/height is required.

id.description:
Caller-assigned identifier, unique within the array
(duplicate ids are rejected as `invalid_options`). Echoed
verbatim as `target_id` on the matching result output — the
addressing contract.

output_format.description: Output format for this variant. When omitted, the source format is preserved.

quality.description: Encode quality (lossy formats only — jpeg / webp / avif). Accepted but ignored for png (lossless).

width.description: Target width in pixels. Required together with height for fit crop/scale; for fit max, at least one of width/height is required.

split.description:
Fan one input file into N outputs across GIF, PDF, audio, and
video MIME families. Single-input — one input file per job, fanned
into N outputs per the per-mime-group catalog. Mirrors the
`merge` / `convert` catalog-split-by-mime-group pattern.

**Three shared modes** (audio + video; the mode discriminator
gates which option applies per the `depends_on` rules in this
schema):
- `interval`: split every N numeric-seconds.
- `count`: split into N equal-duration pieces (integer 2..=200).
- `cut_points`: explicit cut points in numeric-seconds (strictly
  increasing, no duplicates, each > 0 and < probed duration).

**Plus an audio-only `silence` mode** (`beta`, gated by the
`silence_mode_audio` feature): cut at detected silence gaps via
server-side silence detection. Video stays three-mode.

**GIF + PDF use range-based selection** instead of three modes:
- GIF (`image_gif`): `frame_range` REQUIRED + `output_format`
  enum {png,webp,jpg}.
- PDF (`document_pdf`): `page_range` OR `page_groups` (mutually
  exclusive per `depends_on`).

**Wire format**: numeric seconds (floats allowed for sub-second
precision). NOT ISO 8601 duration strings. Matches common
video-tooling conventions.

**200-output hard cap** (`OperationResult.outputs[].maxItems:
200`). Preflight rejects
requests exceeding this BEFORE work as `invalid_options` (422).
Cap math by mode:
- `interval`: `ceil(probed_duration / interval) <= 200`
- `count`: enforced by the option's `min: 2 max: 200` range
- `cut_points`: `cut_points.len() + 1 <= 200`
- `silence` (audio): resolved segment count (detected gaps + 1)
  `<= 200`; preflight rejects as `invalid_options` (422)
- PDF: resolved page count after expansion `<= 200`
  (cross-ref [`WgCqnMRa`](https://trello.com/c/WgCqnMRa))
- GIF: resolved frame count after expansion `<= 200`

**Output naming**: 3-digit zero-padded — `output-001` ..
`output-200`. Matches the convert PDF→N precedent.

**Output envelope binding**:
- `image_gif`: `PositionIndexed` (frame stream-position 0-based
  ordinal)
- `document_pdf`: `PageIndexed` (1-based gapless page index)
- `audio`: `PositionIndexed` (cut stream-position ordinal)
- `video`: `PositionIndexed` (cut stream-position ordinal)

**`precision` flag** (audio + video only): `fast` (default) =
packet-boundary approximate cuts; `exact` = re-encode-aligned
precise cuts. Mime_group scoping is the gate — precision does
NOT apply to GIF or PDF (frame extraction is exact and PDF page
selection is exact by definition).

**Long-form video** routes to a separate long-form processing
path (same `split` OperationType; routing via `processing_class`).
No schema-side discriminator beyond MIME detection +
`processing_class`.

`availability: beta` for the `audio` and `video` mime_groups
(shape-stable + opt-in, MUST NOT 422). Video activates **both
classes**: `video.processing_class.short_form` AND `long_form` are
`beta`. The `image_gif` and `document_pdf` mime_groups stay
`availability: planned` until their cross-repo backend support ships;
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) for the still-planned
groups/classes until then.

silence_mode_audio.description:
Silence-detect cut mode for audio (`mode: silence` —
server-side silence detection). `beta`: shape-stable + opt-in.
The mode ships dark until the API regenerates enum validation
to accept `mode: silence`. The `silence` enum value carries a
matching `per_value_availability: beta` on `audio.mode`.

cut_points.description:
Cut points in numeric seconds (float, sub-second allowed).
Array entries; NOT a comma-separated string. MUST be
strictly increasing (preflight-validated against probe);
each entry > 0 and < probed duration. `uniqueItems: true`
catches accidental duplicates at the OpenAPI layer; strict
monotonicity and the < probed-duration bound are
preflight-checked at workflow-create. Output count =
`cut_points.length + 1`; `maxItems: 199` expresses the
200-output cap declaratively. Only valid when
`mode: cut_points`.

interval.description:
Cut interval in numeric seconds (float, sub-second
allowed). `ceil(probed_duration / interval) <= 200`
preflight rule applies. Only valid when `mode: interval`.

mode.description:
Split strategy:
- `interval`: cut every `interval` seconds.
- `count`: cut into `count` equal-duration pieces.
- `cut_points`: cut at explicit timestamps.
- `silence`: cut at detected silence gaps (audio-only,
  `beta` via the `silence_mode_audio` feature gate).

precision.description:
Cut precision. Under `fast`, audio cuts are
packet-boundary approximate (drift bounded by codec
packet size — typically <= 23ms for AAC, <= 26ms for
MP3). Under `exact`, the server re-encodes to align cuts
precisely at the requested timestamps (slower, larger
output, but sample-accurate).

silence_handling.description:
How detected silence is handled at segment boundaries.
`keep_at_end`: trailing silence stays appended to the
preceding segment (no separate silence-only outputs).
Only valid when `mode: silence`.

silence_min_duration.description:
Minimum duration of a silence gap, in numeric seconds
(sub-second allowed), before it is treated as a cut point.
`min: 0.001` is the strict-positive sub-second idiom
(cf. `interval`). Only valid when `mode: silence`.

silence_threshold_db.description:
Noise floor below which audio counts as silence, in dBFS
(negative; 0.0 = full scale). Strictly negative in
practice; `max: 0.0` is the declarative ceiling (a 0 dB
"silence" floor is degenerate, like the strict bounds
documented in prose for `cut_points`). Only valid when
`mode: silence`.

mode.description:
PDF split strategy:
- `page_range`: select pages via a range expression.
- `page_groups`: group N pages per output.

page_groups.description:
Group N pages per output (e.g. `page_groups: 5` on a
50-page PDF produces 10 outputs of 5 pages each).
Required when `mode: page_groups`. The page-count cap
applies to the resolved group count: `ceil(total_pages /
page_groups) <= 200`.

page_range.description:
Page-range expression (e.g. "1-5", "1,3,5-7", "10-20").
1-based inclusive. Required when `mode: page_range`.
Resolved page count MUST be <= 200 (`invalid_options`
422 if exceeded — cross-ref
[`WgCqnMRa`](https://trello.com/c/WgCqnMRa)).

frame_range.description:
Frame range as `start-end` integer pair (e.g. "1-100").
REQUIRED in V1 — no silent default. Without explicit
range, a 30-second GIF at 24 fps = 720 frames would blow
the 200-output cap on every input. Both start and end are
1-based, inclusive. Resolved frame count MUST be <= 200
(`invalid_options` 422 if exceeded).

output_format.description:
Output image format for each extracted frame. Aspect ratio
and dimensions preserved from the source GIF.

count.description:
Number of equal-duration output pieces. Range 2..=200.
Only valid when `mode: count`.

cut_points.description:
Cut points in numeric seconds (float, sub-second allowed).
Array entries; NOT a comma-separated string. Strictly
increasing (preflight-validated against probe); each entry
> 0 and < probed duration. `uniqueItems: true` catches
accidental duplicates at the OpenAPI layer; strict
monotonicity and the < probed-duration bound are
preflight-checked at workflow-create. Output count =
`cut_points.length + 1`; `maxItems: 199` expresses the
200-output cap declaratively. Only valid when
`mode: cut_points`.

interval.description:
Cut interval in numeric seconds. `ceil(probed_duration /
interval) <= 200`. Only valid when `mode: interval`.

mode.description:
Split strategy:
- `interval`: cut every `interval` seconds.
- `count`: cut into `count` equal-duration pieces.
- `cut_points`: cut at explicit timestamps.

precision.description:
Cut precision. Under `fast`, video cuts are keyframe-
aligned (drift bounded by GOP size — typically up to 1-2
seconds depending on encoder). Under `exact`, the backend
re-encodes to align cuts precisely at the requested
timestamps (slower, larger output, but frame-accurate).

text_watermark.description:
Render a text overlay onto an image using bundled Liberation Sans
(SIL OFL). Single-input — text comes from options, not from a file
input. Source capped at 100 megapixels in `single` mode. `tiled`
mode keeps the 25-megapixel canvas bound (see `tile_spacing` /
`watermark_mode`).

Two rendering modes:
- `single`: one label rendered at the anchor + margin position.
- `tiled`: text tiled across the source image (Cinavia / Adobe Stock
  pattern); rotation default -45° and tile_spacing controls density.

Stable today for image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp inputs. TIFF
(image/tiff) and BMP (image/bmp) inputs are now `stable` too (flipped
planned->stable 2026-06-30 on the lambdas staging proof, UANjD38A /
#296) via the parallel `image_tiff` / `image_bmp` mime_groups. Output
mirrors the input format (`produces: same_as_input`), so a format is
only advertised when the worker can both decode AND encode it;
HEIC/AVIF (no encoder) and SVG (cannot re-emit svg from a rasterised
composite) stay out of scope.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position. For watermark_mode: single, the text
label is placed at this anchor + margin_x/margin_y offset.
For watermark_mode: tiled, anchor is ignored (tiles fill the
source image).

color.description:
Text colour as hex RGB (#RRGGBB) or RGBA (#RRGGBBAA).
Default is white with 50% alpha.

font_family.description:
Font family. V2.0 ships with bundled Liberation Sans only;
user-uploaded fonts are a future extension (separate ticket).

font_size.description: Font size in pixels. Clamped to range.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px") or
percentage of base width (e.g. "5%"). Direction is
anchor-relative. Ignored when watermark_mode: tiled.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single only).
When the anchor is `center` the centre is the centre — there
is no anchor-relative direction. Non-zero `margin_x`
(anything other than the default `"0px"` or the equivalent
`"0%"`) combined with `anchor: center` is rejected by the
API at workflow-create time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).
To nudge a roughly-centred label, pick a peripheral anchor
(e.g. `top_left`) and use `margin_x` / `margin_y` to
position it. Not applicable to watermark_mode: tiled, where
anchor + margin are ignored entirely.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
base height. Ignored when watermark_mode: tiled.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single only).
Same rejection rule as `margin_x` — non-zero `margin_y` with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

opacity.description:
Text overlay opacity. Default 1.0 — the alpha channel of
`color` already encodes transparency, so opacity is usually
left at 1.0 and transparency is set via the color hex.

rotation.description:
Rotation angle in degrees. Default -45° suits tiled mode
(angled diagonal pattern). Clamped to range.

tile_spacing.description:
Spacing between tiled labels in pixels. Defaults to font_size
when omitted. Only applies when watermark_mode is tiled.

watermark_mode.description:
Rendering mode:
- `single`: one label placed at the anchor + margin offset.
- `tiled`: repeated tiles across the entire source image at
  the rotation angle.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position. For watermark_mode: single, the text
label is placed at this anchor + margin_x/margin_y offset.
For watermark_mode: tiled, anchor is ignored (tiles fill the
source image).

Default is white with 50% alpha.
