thumbnail.description: Resize an image to specific dimensions, or extract a preview frame/page from a video or document. For images this is a full resizer (1-16384px), not just small previews.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). Optional — see `width` for the omitted-default behaviour.

page.description: 1-based index of the printed page to render, after print-layout pagination is applied. For office formats this is the page in the document's print layout — NOT a 1:1 sheet/slide index: a spreadsheet sheet may span multiple printed pages and multi-sheet workbooks paginate their sheets in order. For PDF inputs it is the PDF page number directly.

quality.description: Output quality for lossy thumbnail formats (jpg/webp): higher = better quality, larger file. Ignored for png (lossless).

source.description: page = render a specific page/slide, cover = extract cover image (EPUB only, ignored for other formats)

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). Optional — when omitted the server applies an aspect-preserving default (both omitted → longest edge 320px; only one of width/height provided → the other is derived from the source aspect ratio).

background.description: Background colour as a 6-digit hex (`#RRGGBB`, e.g. `#ffffff`) for a transparent image (e.g. PNG/WebP with alpha) resized to an opaque JPG thumbnail — the alpha-flatten fill colour. Mirrors convert.image.background. HEX ONLY: a non-hex value (e.g. a CSS named colour) is rejected rather than advertising an inert free-form string. When omitted, transparency is flattened onto white. JPG output only: PNG/WebP keep alpha, so the flatten (and this option) do not apply.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). For image input this is a full resize. Optional — see `width` for the omitted-default (aspect-preserving) behaviour.

quality.description: Output quality for lossy thumbnail formats (jpg / webp / png): higher = better quality, larger file. For PNG, `quality` < 100 applies lossy palette quantization (imagequant — transparency preserved); `quality: 100` keeps the PNG lossless. Per-format effective default (server-resolved when `quality` is unset, per ADR-0020 D1): PNG → 70 (more aggressive — thumbnails target small files), jpg/webp → 85. The schema `default` is the nominal jpg/webp value (85); the FE shows 70 as the initial PNG slider value.

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). For image input this is a full resize, not just a small preview. Optional — when omitted the server applies an aspect-preserving default (both omitted → longest edge 320px; only one of width/height provided → the other is derived from the source aspect ratio), so non-square images are never distorted.

height.description: Target height in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). Rounded down to an even number. Optional — see `width` for the omitted-default behaviour.

quality.description: Output quality for lossy thumbnail formats (jpg/webp): higher = better quality, larger file. Ignored for png (lossless).

width.description: Target width in pixels (1-16384; width*height <= 16MP). Rounded down to an even number. Optional — when omitted the server applies an aspect-preserving default (both omitted → longest edge 320px; only one of width/height provided → the other is derived from the source aspect ratio).

video_text_watermark.description:
Render a text overlay onto a base video using bundled Liberation
Sans (SIL OFL). Single-input — the text and its styling come from
options, not from a file input.

**Dedicated operation, not an extension of `text_watermark`.**
`text_watermark` renders onto images (single-pass); per-frame video
text rendering requires a re-encode (overlay = pixel modification,
no stream-copy escape). Different runtime.

**Audio passthrough.** The base video's audio stream is preserved
unchanged (stream-copied) — the watermark applies to the visual
track only.

Two rendering modes (mirrors `text_watermark`):
- `single`: one label rendered at the anchor + margin position.
- `tiled`: text tiled across the source frames at the rotation
  angle.

`availability: planned` until the cross-repo backend support ships;
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until then.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position. For `watermark_mode: single`, the
text label is placed at this anchor + margin_x/margin_y
offset on each frame. For `watermark_mode: tiled`, anchor
is ignored (tiles fill the frame).

color.description:
Text colour as hex RGB (#RRGGBB) or RGBA (#RRGGBBAA).
Default is white with 50% alpha. Same vocabulary as
`text_watermark.image.color`.

font_size.description: Font size in pixels. Clamped to range.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px")
or percentage of video frame width (e.g. "5%").
Anchor-relative. Ignored when `watermark_mode: tiled`.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single
only). Non-zero `margin_x` combined with `anchor: center`
is rejected by the API at workflow-create time as
`invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
video frame height. Ignored when `watermark_mode: tiled`.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single
only). Same rejection rule as `margin_x`.

opacity.description:
Text overlay opacity. Default 1.0 — the alpha channel of
`color` already encodes transparency, so opacity is
usually left at 1.0 and transparency is set via the color
hex.

rotation.description:
Rotation angle in degrees. Default -45° suits tiled mode
(angled diagonal pattern). Clamped to range.

tile_spacing.description:
Spacing between tiled labels in pixels. Defaults to
font_size when omitted. Only applies when watermark_mode
is tiled.

watermark_mode.description:
Rendering mode:
- `single`: one label placed at the anchor + margin offset
  on each frame.
- `tiled`: repeated tiles across the entire frame at the
  rotation angle.

video_watermark.description:
Apply an image overlay onto a base video
asset. Multi-input role-based:
exactly one input with `role: base` (the source video) + exactly
one with `role: overlay` (the watermark image). Each input is a
a multi-input source — an external_import handle, a vault connection,
or an upstream `job_output` (uploads are NOT referenced directly
inside inputs[]). To use an uploaded base or overlay, feed it through
a `passthrough` source job and reference it via `{ type: job_output,
from: <id> }` (per ticket 4som89Uh).

**Dedicated operation, not an extension of `image_watermark`.**
`image_watermark` handles image-on-image overlay only.
`video_watermark` requires an overlay + re-encode (no stream-copy escape — overlay = pixel modification).

**Audio passthrough.** The base video's audio stream is preserved
unchanged (stream-copied) — the watermark applies to the visual
track only.

Activated to `availability: beta` (Wave A, ticket
[`c3uthIP4`](https://trello.com/c/c3uthIP4)): the cross-repo backend
is live (API accepts + publishes via the operations SNS
topic), so the operation and its `short_form` processing class are
shape-stable + opt-in and MUST NOT return `feature_not_available`.
`long_form` stays `planned` (its long-form path is not yet
provisioned) and the `multi_overlay_stack` feature stays `planned`
(backend support unconfirmed); requesting those planned sub-features
DOES still return `feature_not_available` until they are separately
activated.

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via per-input placement options.
Backend support not yet confirmed; tagged planned. Mirrors
the same-named feature on `image_watermark`.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position on each video frame. Same vocabulary
as `image_watermark.image` and `text_watermark.image`.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px")
or percentage of video frame width (e.g. "5%"). Direction
is anchor-relative — positive moves toward the frame
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** When the anchor is `center`
the centre is the centre — there is no anchor-relative
direction. Non-zero `margin_x` combined with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422). Same rule as the
other watermark operations.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
video frame height. Anchor-relative; positive moves toward
the frame centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as
`margin_x`.

opacity.description:
Overlay opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).
Clamped to range.

overlay_width.description:
Optional overlay width as pixels or percentage of video
frame width. Aspect ratio preserved. Omit to use the
original overlay size.
